info.name: Kram API
info.description: Complete API collection for Kram Education Management System. Updated with Class Divisions API for simple class organization (dual support for basic and advanced academic management).

request.description: Refresh the JWT access token. Requires valid token.
Activate account for institutional users created by admin. Used on first login to change temporary password.

Map a parent to their child using the child's Kram ID. For parent users only.

request.description:
Create a new institution. Only super_admin can create institutions.

**Code Field:**
- Optional: If not provided, code will be auto-generated from institution name
- If provided: Must be 2-4 uppercase alphanumeric characters and unique

**Type Options:**
- SCHOOL
- COLLEGE
- UNIVERSITY
- INSTITUTE

response.name: Institution Created (auto-generated code)

request.description:
Get the ID format configuration for an institution. Returns the templates used for generating admission numbers, roll numbers, and employee IDs.

**Available Placeholders:**
- `{YEAR}` - Full year (2025)
- `{YY}` - 2-digit year (25)
- `{MONTH}` or `{MM}` - Month (01-12)
- `{INST}` - Institution code
- `{COURSE}` or `{BRANCH}` - Course code
- `{SECTION}` - Section letter
- `{SEQ:N}` - Sequence number padded to N digits

Update the ID format configuration for an institution.

**Template Formats:**

| Style | Admission Number | Roll Number |
|-------|------------------|-------------|
| Standard | `{YEAR}/{COURSE}/{SEQ:5}` | `{COURSE}-{SECTION}-{SEQ:3}` |
| Compact | `{YY}{COURSE}{SEQ:4}` | `{COURSE}{SECTION}{SEQ:3}` |
| Institution Prefixed | `{INST}/{YEAR}/{COURSE}/{SEQ:4}` | `{INST}-{COURSE}-{SECTION}-{SEQ:3}` |
| Legacy | `ADM{SEQ:6}` | `ROLL{SEQ:5}` |

**Sequence Reset Policies:**
- `YEARLY` - Reset sequence at start of each year
- `MONTHLY` - Reset sequence at start of each month
- `NEVER` - Continuously incrementing sequence
Preview what an ID would look like with a given template. Useful for testing templates before saving.

request.description: Unlock a locked user account
Get admin dashboard statistics
Get teacher performance analytics
Get attendance trends for the institution
Get grade distribution across the institution
Get performance metrics by class
Get financial overview for the institution
Get system alerts and notifications

**Unified User Creation API** - Creates any user type with role-specific profile in one step.

**Role IDs:**
- 1: Student (studentData optional)
- 2: Teacher (teacherData optional)
- 3: Parent (requires parentData with childKramid)
- 4: Staff (requires staffData with staffType + designation)
- 5: Admin
- 6: Super Admin

**Auto-Generated Fields:**
- Kram ID (always)
- Temporary password (if not provided)
- Admission Number (for students)
- Roll Number (for students with section)
- Employee ID (for teachers/staff)

**Example payloads:**

**Student (roleId: 1) - Minimal:**
```json
{
  "firstName": "John",
  "lastName": "Doe",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "roleId": 1,
  "institutionId": 1,
  "studentData": {
    "courseId": 1,
    "section": "A"
  }
}
```

**Teacher (roleId: 2) - Minimal:**
```json
{
  "firstName": "Jane",
  "lastName": "Smith",
  "email": "jane@example.com",
  "roleId": 2,
  "institutionId": 1,
  "teacherData": {
    "designation": "Professor"
  }
}
```

**Parent (roleId: 3):**
```json
{
  "parentData": {
    "childKramid": "EDU-S-2024-000001",
    "relation": "FATHER"
  }
}
```

**Staff (roleId: 4):**
```json
{
  "staffData": {
    "staffType": "ADMINISTRATIVE",
    "designation": "Office Manager"
  }
}
```

request.description: Create a new examination.

query.description: Filter by status (DRAFT, SCHEDULED, ONGOING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED)

request.description: Get all examinations created by the teacher with result statistics.
Get detailed information about a specific examination including all results.
Update an existing examination.
Delete an examination.

Enter or update exam result for a single student.

**Features:**
- Automatically calculates grade based on marks
- Validates marks don't exceed total marks
- Verifies student enrollment in course
- Updates existing result if already present

**Fields:**
- `studentId` (required): Student ID
- `marksObtained` (optional): Marks scored (0 to totalMarks)
- `isAbsent` (optional): Mark student as absent
- `remarks` (optional): Additional comments

Enter exam results for multiple students at once. This is useful for entering marks for an entire class after grading.

**Features:**
- Process multiple students in one request
- Automatically updates existing results
- Returns success and error counts
- Validates each student's enrollment
- Calculates grades automatically

**Response includes:**
- Total records processed
- Success count
- Error count (if any)
- Detailed results for each student
- Error details for failed records

Get all results for an examination with comprehensive statistics.

**Response includes:**
- Examination details
- Statistics:
  - Total students
  - Evaluated/Pending counts
  - Absent/Present counts
  - Average marks
  - Highest/Lowest marks
  - Pass count and percentage
- List of all student results with:
  - Student name and email
  - Marks obtained
  - Grade
  - Absent status
  - Remarks
  - Evaluator name
  - Evaluation timestamp

Update an existing exam result. Useful for corrections or re-evaluations.

**Updatable Fields:**
- `marksObtained`: Update marks
- `isAbsent`: Change absent status
- `remarks`: Update or add comments

**Features:**
- Automatically recalculates grade
- Updates evaluator and timestamp
- Validates marks against total marks

Delete an exam result. Use with caution as this action is permanent.

**Authorization:**
- Only the teacher who created the exam can delete results

**Note:**
- This action cannot be undone
- Consider updating the result instead of deleting when possible
Get all question papers created by the teacher.
Create an empty question paper for an examination. Add sections and questions separately.

Create a complete question paper with sections and questions in one request.

**Question Types:**
- MCQ, MCQ_MULTI, TRUE_FALSE, FILL_BLANK
- SHORT_ANSWER, LONG_ANSWER, NUMERICAL, MATCH_FOLLOWING

**Difficulty Levels:** EASY, MEDIUM, HARD
Get question paper by examination ID with all sections and questions.
Get question paper by its ID.

Update question paper details.

**Status Options:** DRAFT, READY, PUBLISHED, ARCHIVED
Publish a question paper. Once published, students can view it (without answers).
Delete a question paper. Cannot delete published papers.
Add a new section to a question paper.
Delete a section and all its questions.

query.description: Maximum number of pending submissions to return (default: 10)

request.description:
Get pending submissions that need grading. This is a Phase 1 'medicine' API that shows actionable items for teachers.

**Response includes:**
- List of submissions waiting for grading (oldest first)
- Total count of pending submissions
- Average grading time
- Priority levels (high/medium/low) based on wait time

**Priority Levels:**
- High: Waiting 3+ days
- Medium: Waiting 1-2 days
- Low: Waiting < 1 day

query.description: Maximum number of at-risk students to return (default: 20)

request.description:
Get students who are at risk and need attention. This is a Phase 1 'medicine' API that provides actionable insights and suggested interventions.

**Risk Analysis includes:**
- Missing assignments
- Low attendance rate
- Inactive for extended period
- Low performance

**Risk Levels:**
- High: Risk score >= 50 (urgent intervention needed)
- Medium: Risk score >= 25 (attention required)
- Low: Risk score > 0 (monitor closely)

**Suggested Actions:**
- Schedule 1-on-1 meeting
- Send intervention message
- Offer deadline extension
- Share learning resources
- Assign peer mentor

Mark attendance for a single student in a section.

**Status Options:**
- PRESENT: Student attended the class
- ABSENT: Student was absent
- LATE: Student arrived late
- EXCUSED: Student absence was excused

**Notes:**
- If attendance already exists for the student on this date, it will be updated
- Teacher must be assigned to the section to mark attendance
- Student must be enrolled in the course

Mark attendance for multiple students in a section at once. This is useful for marking attendance for an entire class.

**Features:**
- Mark attendance for multiple students in one request
- Automatically updates existing attendance records
- Returns success and error counts
- Validates student enrollment for each record

**Response includes:**
- Total records processed
- Success count
- Error count (if any)
- Detailed results for each student
- Error details for failed records

Update an existing attendance record. This is useful for correcting mistakes or updating status based on new information (e.g., medical certificate received).

**Updatable Fields:**
- status: Change attendance status
- remarks: Add or update remarks

**Authorization:**
- Only the teacher assigned to the section can update attendance

Delete an attendance record. This should be used carefully and only when necessary (e.g., attendance was marked for wrong date/student).

**Authorization:**
- Only the teacher assigned to the section can delete attendance

**Note:**
- This action is permanent and cannot be undone
- Consider updating the record instead of deleting when possible

query.description: Filter by status: PRESENT, ABSENT, LATE, EXCUSED
Sort by: date, student, section, status (default: date)

request.description:
Query and retrieve attendance records with comprehensive filtering, pagination, and sorting options.

**Use Cases:**
- Review all attendance marked for a specific date/date range
- Find attendance records for a specific student across classes
- Filter by section to see all attendance for a class
- Search for all ABSENT records to follow up
- Generate custom attendance reports

**Query Parameters (all optional):**
- `sectionId`: Filter by specific class section
- `studentId`: Filter by specific student
- `startDate`: Filter from this date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `endDate`: Filter until this date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `status`: Filter by PRESENT, ABSENT, LATE, or EXCUSED
- `limit`: Records per page (default: 50, max: 100)
- `offset`: Pagination offset (default: 0)
- `sortBy`: Sort field - date, student, section, status (default: date)
- `sortOrder`: asc or desc (default: desc)

**Response includes:**
- Total count of matching records
- Pagination info (limit, offset)
- Array of attendance records with:
  - Student details (name, uuid, admission number)
  - Section details (name, subject, semester)
  - Attendance status and remarks
  - Timestamp (markedAt)

**Authorization:**
- Only returns attendance for sections assigned to this teacher

Generate report cards for multiple students at once. This is useful for generating report cards for an entire class or section.

**Request Body Options (all optional, at least one filter recommended):**
- `sectionId`: Generate for all students in a specific section (teacher must be assigned)
- `courseId`: Generate for all students in a course
- `semesterId`: Specific semester (defaults to current/most recent)
- `studentIds`: Array of specific student IDs
- `includeExamDetails`: Include exam-wise breakdown (default: true)

**Response includes:**
- Array of complete report cards for each student
- Summary statistics:
  - Total students, generated/failed counts
  - Average SGPA and attendance
  - Pass/fail counts
  - Grade distribution
- Student summaries for quick overview
- Batch ID for reference

**Authorization:**
- Teacher must be assigned to the section (for sectionId filter)
- Admins can generate for any students

query.description: Filter by grade level / class (e.g., 10, 12, Grade 5)
Filter by section (e.g., A, B, C)

request.description: Get comprehensive dashboard statistics including GPA, attendance, class rank, and assignments due count.

query.description: Filter by status: pending, submitted, graded

request.description: Get student's recent assignments and tests with submission status, grades, and scores.
Get published question paper for an examination. Only available for published papers. Correct answers and hints are hidden from students.
Get subject-wise performance trends over time for chart visualization.

query.description: Semester ID (optional, defaults to current semester)

request.description: Get monthly attendance history for bar chart visualization.
Get detailed subject performance with teachers, grades, and next test dates.
Get upcoming tests, assignments, and events for the student.

query.description: Specific semester ID (optional, defaults to current/most recent)

request.description:
Generate a comprehensive report card for a student.

**Response includes:**
- **Student Info**: Name, Kram ID, admission number, roll number, course, section
- **Semester Info**: Semester name, number, academic year, dates
- **Subject Records**: Subject-wise marks, grades, grade points, teacher remarks
- **Exam Summaries**: Exam-wise marks, percentages, grades, ranks
- **Attendance Summary**: Total/attended/absent classes, percentage, status
- **Performance Summary**: SGPA, CGPA, class rank, percentile, overall status
- **Remarks**: Teacher comments, strengths, areas for improvement

**Access Control:**
- Students can only view their own report cards
- Parents can view their children's report cards
- Teachers/Admins can view any student's report card

Get academic records for the student.
Get attendance records for the student.
Get all courses/programs. Filter by institution, status, or degree type.
Get a single course with its subjects
Get detailed sections for a specific course with year/semester breakdown

Get all class sections (subject-based).

These are sections for specific subjects in a semester, assigned to a teacher.

Example response:
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "sectionName": "A",
  "maxCapacity": 60,
  "currentEnrollment": 55,
  "subject": { "name": "Data Structures", "code": "CS201" },
  "course": { "name": "B.Sc. Computer Science" },
  "semester": { "name": "Semester 3" },
  "teacher": { "name": "Dr. Sharma" }
}
```

Get all class divisions for a course (simple class organization).

Performance optimized with pagination and selective field loading. Target response time < 50ms.

Example response:
```json
{
  "message": "Class divisions retrieved successfully",
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "sectionName": "A",
      "maxCapacity": 50,
      "currentEnrollment": 25,
      "roomNumber": "101",
      "status": "ACTIVE",
      "course": { "id": 1, "name": "Class I", "code": "CLS1" },
      "teacher": { "id": 1, "name": "Ms. Smith", "email": "smith@school.edu" },
      "createdAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "meta": { "total": 3, "page": 1, "limit": 50, "totalPages": 1 }
}
```

Create a new class division for basic student organization.

**Required fields:**
- courseId: Course this division belongs to
- sectionName: Section identifier (e.g., "A", "B", "Alpha")

**Optional fields:**
- teacherId: Class teacher assignment
- maxCapacity: Maximum students (default: 50)
- roomNumber: Assigned room
- status: ACTIVE or INACTIVE (default: ACTIVE)

**Validation:**
- Prevents duplicate section names within same course
- Validates teacher and course existence
- Institution-based access control

Update an existing class division.

**All fields are optional:**
- sectionName: Update section name
- teacherId: Change class teacher
- maxCapacity: Update capacity
- roomNumber: Change room assignment
- status: Change status (ACTIVE/INACTIVE)

**Validation:**
- Prevents duplicate section names within same course
- Validates new teacher assignment
- Institution-based access control

Delete (soft delete) a class division.

**Behavior:**
- Sets status to INACTIVE (soft delete)
- Cannot delete divisions with assigned students
- Preserves data for historical records

**Access Control:**
- Institution-based access control
- Admin/Super Admin roles required

Get all subjects. Optionally filter by courseId, institutionId, or status.

**Query Parameters:**
- courseId (optional) - Filter by course
- institutionId (optional) - Filter by institution
- status (optional) - Filter by status
Get all subjects for a specific course/program

  "id": 1,
  "title": "Important: Semester End Examination Schedule",
  "content": "The semester end examinations will commence from March 15th, 2026.",
